title: Frankl's Union-Closed Sets Conjecture
subtitle: A non-entropic 2/5 bound for k≤4, and a no-go theorem for chamber-marginal LPs at k=5
summary: Two precisely-scoped results on Frankl's 1979 conjecture: (1) any union-closed family with a minimal nonempty member of size at most 4 has max p_x ≥ 2/5, exceeding the universal entropy bound on this restricted class; (2) every linear proof using only edge-mass bad statistics and chamber marginals on the residual k=5 case has exact value (185−32B)/425, dead on B ∈ (77/128, 75/119]. Closing the gap requires a coupling theorem, not an LP refinement.
